tEn: WaveNet: A Generative Model for Raw Audio
sEn: WaveNet is a deep neural network for generating raw audio waveforms, achieving state-of-the-art text-to-speech performance and realistic music generation.
sZh: WaveNet是一种用于生成原始音频波形的深度神经网络，在文本转语音任务中达到最先进水平，并能生成逼真的音乐片段。
absEn: This paper introduces WaveNet, a deep neural network for generating raw audio waveforms. The model is fully probabilistic and autoregressive, with the predictive distribution for each audio sample conditioned on all previous ones; nonetheless we show that it can be efficiently trained on data with tens of thousands of samples per second of audio. When applied to text-to-speech, it yields state-of-the-art performance, with human listeners rating it as significantly more natural sounding than the best parametric and concatenative systems for both English and Mandarin. A single WaveNet can capture the characteristics of many different speakers with equal fidelity, and can switch between them by conditioning on the speaker identity. When trained to model music, we find that it generates novel and often highly realistic musical fragments. We also show that it can be employed as a discriminative model, returning promising results for phoneme recognition.
absZh: 本文介绍了WaveNet，一种用于生成原始音频波形的深度神经网络。该模型是完全概率性和自回归的，每个音频样本的预测分布都基于所有之前的样本；尽管如此，我们表明它可以高效地训练每秒包含数万个样本的音频数据。当应用于文本转语音时，它实现了最先进的性能，人类听众评价其自然度显著优于英语和普通话的最佳参数化和拼接系统。单个WaveNet可以以相同的保真度捕捉许多不同说话者的特征，并通过条件化说话者身份在它们之间切换。当训练用于建模音乐时，我们发现它生成了新颖且通常高度逼真的音乐片段。我们还展示了它可以作为判别模型使用，在音素识别方面取得了有希望的结果。

contrib.en: Introduces WaveNet, a deep autoregressive model for raw audio waveforms.
contrib.zh: 提出WaveNet，一种用于原始音频波形的深度自回归模型。

contrib.en: Uses dilated causal convolutions to achieve large receptive fields efficiently.
contrib.zh: 使用扩张因果卷积高效实现大感受野。

contrib.en: Achieves state-of-the-art text-to-speech naturalness for English and Mandarin.
contrib.zh: 在英语和中文文本转语音中达到最先进的自然度。

contrib.en: Single model can generate multiple voices by conditioning on speaker identity.
contrib.zh: 单个模型可通过条件化说话人身份生成多种声音。

contrib.en: Generates novel and realistic music fragments when trained on music data.
contrib.zh: 在音乐数据上训练时生成新颖且逼真的音乐片段。

contrib.en: Adapts to discriminative tasks like phoneme recognition with strong results.
contrib.zh: 适应音素识别等判别任务并取得强结果。

limits.en: Limited receptive field (e.g., 300 ms) causes lack of long-range coherence in free-form speech.
limits.zh: 有限感受野（如300毫秒）导致自由形式语音缺乏长程连贯性。

limits.en: Text-to-speech prosody can be unnatural without explicit F0 conditioning.
limits.zh: 文本转语音的韵律在没有显式F0条件化时可能不自然。

limits.en: Music generation lacks long-range consistency across seconds.
limits.zh: 音乐生成在数秒内缺乏长程一致性。

limits.en: Softmax over 256 quantized values limits audio fidelity compared to 16-bit.
limits.zh: 256量化值的Softmax相比16位限制了音频保真度。

limits.en: Training and inference are computationally expensive for very long sequences.
limits.zh: 对于极长序列，训练和推理计算成本高。

items.en: This paper introduces WaveNet, a deep neural network for generating raw audio waveforms. The model is fully probabilistic and autoregressive, with the predictive distribution for each audio sample conditioned on all previous ones; nonetheless we show that it can be efficiently trained on data with tens of thousands of samples per second of audio. When applied to text-to-speech, it yields state-of-the-art performance, with human listeners rating it as significantly more natural sounding than the best parametric and concatenative systems for both English and Mandarin. A single WaveNet can capture the characteristics of many different speakers with equal fidelity, and can switch between them by conditioning on the speaker identity. When trained to model music, we find that it generates novel and often highly realistic musical fragments. We also show that it can be employed as a discriminative model, returning promising results for phoneme recognition.
items.zh: 本文介绍了WaveNet，一种用于生成原始音频波形的深度神经网络。该模型是完全概率性和自回归的，每个音频样本的预测分布以所有先前样本为条件；尽管如此，我们表明它可以在每秒数万个音频样本的数据上高效训练。当应用于文本到语音时，它实现了最先进的性能，人类听众认为其自然度显著优于英语和普通话的最佳参数化和拼接系统。单个WaveNet可以以相同的保真度捕捉许多不同说话者的特征，并通过以说话者身份为条件在它们之间切换。当训练用于建模音乐时，我们发现它生成了新颖且通常高度逼真的音乐片段。我们还表明，它可以作为判别模型使用，在音素识别方面取得了有希望的结果。

items.en: This work explores raw audio generation techniques, inspired by recent advances in neural autoregressive generative models that model complex distributions such as images (van den Oord et al., 2016a; b) and text (Józefowicz et al., 2016). Modeling joint probabilities over pixels or words using neural architectures as products of conditional distributions yields state-of-the-art generation.
items.zh: 这项工作探索了原始音频生成技术，灵感来自最近在神经自回归生成模型方面的进展，这些模型对图像（van den Oord等人，2016a; b）和文本（Józefowicz等人，2016）等复杂分布进行建模。使用神经架构将像素或单词上的联合概率建模为条件分布的乘积，产生了最先进的生成结果。

items.en: Remarkably, these architectures are able to model distributions over thousands of random variables (e.g. 64×\times64 pixels as in PixelRNN (van den Oord et al., 2016a)). The question this paper addresses is whether similar approaches can succeed in generating wideband raw audio waveforms, which are signals with very high temporal resolution, at least 16,000 samples per second (see Fig. 1).
items.zh: 值得注意的是，这些架构能够对数千个随机变量（例如PixelRNN中的64×64像素（van den Oord等人，2016a））的分布进行建模。本文解决的问题是，类似的方法是否能够成功生成宽带原始音频波形，这些波形具有非常高的时间分辨率，至少每秒16,000个样本（见图1）。

items.capEn: Figure 1: A second of generated speech.

items.en: This paper introduces WaveNet, an audio generative model based on the PixelCNN (van den Oord et al., 2016a; b) architecture. The main contributions of this work are as follows:
items.zh: 本文介绍了WaveNet，一种基于PixelCNN（van den Oord等人，2016a; b）架构的音频生成模型。本研究的主要贡献如下：

items.en: We show that WaveNets can generate raw speech signals with subjective naturalness never before reported in the field of text-to-speech (TTS), as assessed by human raters.
items.zh: 我们表明，WaveNet可以生成原始语音信号，其主观自然度在文本到语音（TTS）领域从未有过，经人类评估者评定。

items.en: In order to deal with long-range temporal dependencies needed for raw audio generation, we develop new architectures based on dilated causal convolutions, which exhibit very large receptive fields.
items.zh: 为了处理原始音频生成所需的长期时间依赖性，我们开发了基于扩张因果卷积的新架构，该架构具有非常大的感受野。

items.en: We show that when conditioned on a speaker identity, a single model can be used to generate different voices.
items.zh: 我们表明，当以说话者身份为条件时，单个模型可用于生成不同的声音。

items.en: The same architecture shows strong results when tested on a small speech recognition dataset, and is promising when used to generate other audio modalities such as music.
items.zh: 相同的架构在小型语音识别数据集上测试时显示出强劲的结果，并且在用于生成其他音频模态（如音乐）时也很有前景。

items.en: We believe that WaveNets provide a generic and flexible framework for tackling many applications that rely on audio generation (e.g. TTS, music, speech enhancement, voice conversion, source separation).
items.zh: 我们相信WaveNet为处理许多依赖音频生成的应用（例如TTS、音乐、语音增强、语音转换、源分离）提供了一个通用且灵活的框架。

items.en: In this paper we introduce a new generative model operating directly on the raw audio waveform. The joint probability of a waveform 𝐱={x1,…,xT}𝐱subscript𝑥1…subscript𝑥𝑇\mathbf{x}=\{x_{1},\dots,x_{T}\} is factorised as a product of conditional probabilities as follows:
items.zh: 在本文中，我们介绍了一种直接对原始音频波形进行操作的新生成模型。波形𝐱={x1,…,xT}的联合概率被分解为条件概率的乘积，如下所示：

items.en: Each audio sample xtsubscript𝑥𝑡x_{t} is therefore conditioned on the samples at all previous timesteps.
items.zh: 因此，每个音频样本xt以所有先前时间步的样本为条件。

items.en: Similarly to PixelCNNs (van den Oord et al., 2016a; b), the conditional probability distribution is modelled by a stack of convolutional layers. There are no pooling layers in the network, and the output of the model has the same time dimensionality as the input. The model outputs a categorical distribution over the next value xtsubscript𝑥𝑡x_{t} with a softmax layer and it is optimized to maximize the log-likelihood of the data w.r.t. the parameters. Because log-likelihoods are tractable, we tune hyper-parameters on a validation set and can easily measure if the model is overfitting or underfitting.
items.zh: 与PixelCNN（van den Oord等人，2016a; b）类似，条件概率分布由一系列卷积层建模。网络中没有池化层，模型的输出与输入具有相同的时间维度。模型通过softmax层输出下一个值xt的分类分布，并优化以最大化数据相对于参数的对数似然。由于对数似然是可处理的，我们在验证集上调整超参数，并可以轻松测量模型是否过拟合或欠拟合。

items.capEn: Figure 2: Visualization of a stack of causal convolutional layers.
items.capZh: 图2：因果卷积层的堆叠可视化。

items.en: The main ingredient of WaveNet are causal convolutions. By using causal convolutions, we make sure the model cannot violate the ordering in which we model the data: the prediction p​(xt+1∣x1,…,xt)𝑝conditionalsubscript𝑥𝑡1subscript𝑥1…subscript𝑥𝑡p\left(x_{t+1}\mid x_{1},...,x_{t}\right) emitted by the model at timestep t𝑡t cannot depend on any of the future timesteps xt+1,xt+2,…,xTsubscript𝑥𝑡1subscript𝑥𝑡2…subscript𝑥𝑇x_{t+1},x_{t+2},\dots,x_{T} as shown in Fig. 2. For images, the equivalent of a causal convolution is a masked convolution (van den Oord et al., 2016a) which can be implemented by constructing a mask tensor and doing an elementwise multiplication of this mask with the convolution kernel before applying it. For 1-D data such as audio one can more easily implement this by shifting the output of a normal convolution by a few timesteps.
items.zh: WaveNet的主要成分是因果卷积。通过使用因果卷积，我们确保模型不能违反数据建模的顺序：模型在时间步t发出的预测p(xt+1∣x1,…,xt)不能依赖于任何未来时间步xt+1,xt+2,…,xT，如图2所示。对于图像，因果卷积的等价物是掩码卷积（van den Oord等人，2016a），可以通过构造掩码张量并在应用之前将该掩码与卷积核逐元素相乘来实现。对于一维数据（如音频），可以通过将普通卷积的输出移动几个时间步来更轻松地实现这一点。

items.en: At training time, the conditional predictions for all timesteps can be made in parallel because all timesteps of ground truth 𝐱𝐱\mathbf{x} are known. When generating with the model, the predictions are sequential: after each sample is predicted, it is fed back into the network to predict the next sample.
items.zh: 在训练时，由于所有时间步的真实𝐱都是已知的，因此可以并行进行所有时间步的条件预测。在使用模型生成时，预测是顺序的：在预测每个样本后，将其反馈回网络以预测下一个样本。

items.en: Because models with causal convolutions do not have recurrent connections, they are typically faster to train than RNNs, especially when applied to very long sequences. One of the problems of causal convolutions is that they require many layers, or large filters to increase the receptive field. For example, in Fig. 2 the receptive field is only 5 (= #layers + filter length - 1). In this paper we use dilated convolutions to increase the receptive field by orders of magnitude, without greatly increasing computational cost.
items.zh: 由于具有因果卷积的模型没有循环连接，因此它们通常比RNN训练更快，尤其是在处理非常长的序列时。因果卷积的一个问题是它们需要许多层或大滤波器来增加感受野。例如，在图2中，感受野仅为5（=层数+滤波器长度-1）。在本文中，我们使用扩张卷积将感受野增加几个数量级，而不会大幅增加计算成本。

items.en: A dilated convolution (also called à trous, or convolution with holes) is a convolution where the filter is applied over an area larger than its length by skipping input values with a certain step. It is equivalent to a convolution with a larger filter derived from the original filter by dilating it with zeros, but is significantly more efficient. A dilated convolution effectively allows the network to operate on a coarser scale than with a normal convolution. This is similar to pooling or strided convolutions, but here the output has the same size as the input. As a special case, dilated convolution with dilation 111 yields the standard convolution. Fig. 3 depicts dilated causal convolutions for dilations 111, 222, 444, and 888. Dilated convolutions have previously been used in various contexts, e.g. signal processing (Holschneider et al., 1989; Dutilleux, 1989), and image segmentation (Chen et al., 2015; Yu & Koltun, 2016).
items.zh: 扩张卷积（也称为à trous或带孔卷积）是一种卷积，其中滤波器通过以一定步长跳过输入值而应用于比其长度更大的区域。它等效于使用通过用零扩张从原始滤波器导出的更大滤波器进行卷积，但效率显著更高。扩张卷积有效地允许网络在比普通卷积更粗糙的尺度上操作。这类似于池化或步长卷积，但这里的输出与输入大小相同。作为特例，扩张率为1的扩张卷积产生标准卷积。图3展示了扩张率为1、2、4和8的扩张因果卷积。扩张卷积先前已在各种上下文中使用，例如信号处理（Holschneider等人，1989；Dutilleux，1989）和图像分割（Chen等人，2015；Yu & Koltun，2016）。

items.capEn: Figure 3: Visualization of a stack of dilated causal convolutional layers.
items.capZh: 图3：扩张因果卷积层的堆叠可视化。

items.en: Stacked dilated convolutions enable networks to have very large receptive fields with just a few layers, while preserving the input resolution throughout the network as well as computational efficiency. In this paper, the dilation is doubled for every layer up to a limit and then repeated: e.g.
items.zh: 堆叠的扩张卷积使网络仅用几层就能拥有非常大的感受野，同时在整个网络中保持输入分辨率以及计算效率。在本文中，每层的扩张率加倍直到一个极限，然后重复：例如

items.en: The intuition behind this configuration is two-fold. First, exponentially increasing the dilation factor results in exponential receptive field growth with depth (Yu & Koltun, 2016). For example each 1,2,4,…,512124…5121,2,4,\dots,512 block has receptive field of size 102410241024, and can be seen as a more efficient and discriminative (non-linear) counterpart of a 1×1024110241\times 1024 convolution. Second, stacking these blocks further increases the model capacity and the receptive field size.
items.zh: 这种配置背后的直觉有两点。首先，指数级增加膨胀因子会导致感受野随深度呈指数级增长（Yu & Koltun, 2016）。例如，每个 1,2,4,…,512 的块具有大小为 1024 的感受野，可以看作是 1×1024 卷积的更高效且更具判别性（非线性）的对应物。其次，堆叠这些块进一步增加了模型容量和感受野大小。

items.en: One approach to modeling the conditional distributions p​(xt∣x1,…,xt−1)𝑝conditionalsubscript𝑥𝑡subscript𝑥1…subscript𝑥𝑡1p\left(x_{t}\mid x_{1},\dots,x_{t-1}\right) over the individual audio samples would be to use a mixture model such as a mixture density network (Bishop, 1994) or mixture of conditional Gaussian scale mixtures (MCGSM) (Theis & Bethge, 2015). However, van den Oord et al. (2016a) showed that a softmax distribution tends to work better, even when the data is implicitly continuous (as is the case for image pixel intensities or audio sample values). One of the reasons is that a categorical distribution is more flexible and can more easily model arbitrary distributions because it makes no assumptions about their shape.
items.zh: 对单个音频样本上的条件分布 p(xt|x1,…,xt−1) 进行建模的一种方法是使用混合模型，例如混合密度网络（Bishop, 1994）或条件高斯尺度混合（MCGSM）（Theis & Bethge, 2015）。然而，van den Oord 等人（2016a）表明，即使数据是隐式连续的（如图像像素强度或音频样本值），softmax 分布往往效果更好。原因之一是分类分布更灵活，更容易建模任意分布，因为它对分布形状不做任何假设。

items.en: Because raw audio is typically stored as a sequence of 16-bit integer values (one per timestep), a softmax layer would need to output 65,536 probabilities per timestep to model all possible values. To make this more tractable, we first apply a μ𝜇\mu-law companding transformation (ITU-T, 1988) to the data, and then quantize it to 256 possible values:
items.zh: 由于原始音频通常存储为 16 位整数值序列（每个时间步一个值），softmax 层需要为每个时间步输出 65,536 个概率才能对所有可能值进行建模。为了使这更易处理，我们首先对数据应用 μ 律压扩变换（ITU-T, 1988），然后将其量化为 256 个可能值：

items.en: where −1<xt<11subscript𝑥𝑡1-1<x_{t}<1 and μ=255𝜇255\mu=255. This non-linear quantization produces a significantly better reconstruction than a simple linear quantization scheme. Especially for speech, we found that the reconstructed signal after quantization sounded very similar to the original.
items.zh: 其中 −1<xt<1 且 μ=255。这种非线性量化产生的重建效果明显优于简单的线性量化方案。特别是对于语音，我们发现量化后的重建信号听起来与原始信号非常相似。

items.en: We use the same gated activation unit as used in the gated PixelCNN (van den Oord et al., 2016b):
items.zh: 我们使用与门控 PixelCNN（van den Oord 等人，2016b）中相同的门控激活单元：

items.en: where ∗∗\ast denotes a convolution operator, ⊙direct-product\odot denotes an element-wise multiplication operator, σ​(⋅)𝜎⋅\sigma(\cdot) is a sigmoid function, k𝑘k is the layer index, f𝑓f and g𝑔g denote filter and gate, respectively, and W𝑊W is a learnable convolution filter. In our initial experiments, we observed that this non-linearity worked significantly better than the rectified linear activation function (Nair & Hinton, 2010) for modeling audio signals.
items.zh: 其中 ∗ 表示卷积算子，⊙ 表示逐元素乘法算子，σ(·) 是 sigmoid 函数，k 是层索引，f 和 g 分别表示滤波器和门，W 是可学习的卷积滤波器。在我们的初步实验中，我们观察到这种非线性在建模音频信号时明显优于修正线性激活函数（Nair & Hinton, 2010）。

items.capEn: Figure 4: Overview of the residual block and the entire architecture.
items.capZh: 图 4：残差块和整个架构的概览。

items.en: Both residual (He et al., 2015) and parameterised skip connections are used throughout the network, to speed up convergence and enable training of much deeper models. In Fig. 4 we show a residual block of our model, which is stacked many times in the network.
items.zh: 残差（He 等人，2015）和参数化的跳跃连接都用于整个网络，以加速收敛并能够训练更深的模型。在图 4 中，我们展示了模型的一个残差块，该块在网络中多次堆叠。

items.en: Given an additional input 𝐡𝐡\mathbf{h}, WaveNets can model the conditional distribution p​(𝐱∣𝐡)𝑝conditional𝐱𝐡p\left(\mathbf{x}\mid\mathbf{h}\right) of the audio given this input. Eq. (1) now becomes
items.zh: 给定额外输入 h，WaveNet 可以建模给定该输入下音频的条件分布 p(x|h)。公式 (1) 现在变为

items.en: By conditioning the model on other input variables, we can guide WaveNet’s generation to produce audio with the required characteristics. For example, in a multi-speaker setting we can choose the speaker by feeding the speaker identity to the model as an extra input. Similarly, for TTS we need to feed information about the text as an extra input.
items.zh: 通过将模型条件设置在其他输入变量上，我们可以引导 WaveNet 的生成以产生具有所需特征的音频。例如，在多说话人设置中，我们可以通过将说话人身份作为额外输入馈送到模型来选择说话人。类似地，对于 TTS，我们需要将文本信息作为额外输入馈送。

items.en: We condition the model on other inputs in two different ways: global conditioning and local conditioning. Global conditioning is characterised by a single latent representation 𝐡𝐡\mathbf{h} that influences the output distribution across all timesteps, e.g. a speaker embedding in a TTS model. The activation function from Eq. (2) now becomes:
items.zh: 我们以两种不同的方式将模型条件设置在其他输入上：全局条件和局部条件。全局条件的特征是一个单一的潜在表示 h，它影响所有时间步的输出分布，例如 TTS 模型中的说话人嵌入。公式 (2) 中的激活函数现在变为：

items.en: where V∗,ksubscript𝑉𝑘V_{*,k} is a learnable linear projection, and the vector V∗,kT​𝐡superscriptsubscript𝑉𝑘𝑇𝐡V_{*,k}^{T}\mathbf{h} is broadcast over the time dimension.
items.zh: 其中 V*,k 是可学习的线性投影，向量 V*,k^T h 在时间维度上广播。

items.en: For local conditioning we have a second timeseries htsubscriptℎ𝑡h_{t}, possibly with a lower sampling frequency than the audio signal, e.g. linguistic features in a TTS model. We first transform this time series using a transposed convolutional network (learned upsampling) that maps it to a new time series 𝐲=f​(𝐡)𝐲𝑓𝐡\mathbf{y}=f(\mathbf{h}) with the same resolution as the audio signal, which is then used in the activation unit as follows:
items.zh: 对于局部条件，我们有第二个时间序列 ht，其采样频率可能低于音频信号，例如 TTS 模型中的语言特征。我们首先使用转置卷积网络（学习上采样）将该时间序列转换为新的时间序列 y = f(h)，其分辨率与音频信号相同，然后在激活单元中使用如下：

items.en: where Vf,k∗𝐲∗subscript𝑉𝑓𝑘𝐲V_{f,k}\ast\mathbf{y} is now a 1×1111\times 1 convolution. As an alternative to the transposed convolutional network, it is also possible to use Vf,k∗𝐡∗subscript𝑉𝑓𝑘𝐡V_{f,k}\ast\mathbf{h} and repeat these values across time. We saw that this worked slightly worse in our experiments.
items.zh: 其中 Vf,k * y 现在是 1×1 卷积。作为转置卷积网络的替代方案，也可以使用 Vf,k * h 并在时间上重复这些值。我们在实验中看到这效果稍差。

items.en: We have already mentioned several different ways to increase the receptive field size of a WaveNet: increasing the number of dilation stages, using more layers, larger filters, greater dilation factors, or a combination thereof. A complementary approach is to use a separate, smaller context stack that processes a long part of the audio signal and locally conditions a larger WaveNet that processes only a smaller part of the audio signal (cropped at the end). One can use multiple context stacks with varying lengths and numbers of hidden units. Stacks with larger receptive fields have fewer units per layer. Context stacks can also have pooling layers to run at a lower frequency. This keeps the computational requirements at a reasonable level and is consistent with the intuition that less capacity is required to model temporal correlations at longer timescales.
items.zh: 我们已经提到了几种增加 WaveNet 感受野大小的方法：增加膨胀阶段的数量、使用更多层、更大的滤波器、更大的膨胀因子，或它们的组合。一种补充方法是使用一个单独的、较小的上下文堆栈来处理音频信号的长部分，并局部条件一个处理音频信号较小部分（在末尾裁剪）的较大 WaveNet。可以使用多个具有不同长度和隐藏单元数量的上下文堆栈。具有较大感受野的堆栈每层有更少的单元。上下文堆栈也可以有池化层以较低频率运行。这使计算需求保持在合理水平，并且与直觉一致，即建模更长时间尺度上的时间相关性需要更少的容量。

items.en: To measure WaveNet’s audio modelling performance, we evaluate it on three different tasks: multi-speaker speech generation (not conditioned on text), TTS, and music audio modelling. We provide samples drawn from WaveNet for these experiments on the accompanying webpage: https://www.deepmind.com/blog/wavenet-generative-model-raw-audio/.
items.zh: 为了衡量 WaveNet 的音频建模性能，我们在三个不同的任务上对其进行评估：多说话人语音生成（不基于文本条件）、TTS 和音乐音频建模。我们在随附的网页上提供了这些实验的 WaveNet 生成样本：https://www.deepmind.com/blog/wavenet-generative-model-raw-audio/。

items.en: For the first experiment we looked at free-form speech generation (not conditioned on text). We used the English multi-speaker corpus from CSTR voice cloning toolkit (VCTK) (Yamagishi, 2012) and conditioned WaveNet only on the speaker. The conditioning was applied by feeding the speaker ID to the model in the form of a one-hot vector. The dataset consisted of 44 hours of data from 109 different speakers.
items.zh: 对于第一个实验，我们研究了自由形式的语音生成（不基于文本条件）。我们使用了来自 CSTR 语音克隆工具包（VCTK）（Yamagishi, 2012）的英语多说话人语料库，并仅将 WaveNet 条件设置在说话人上。通过以独热向量的形式将说话人 ID 馈送到模型来应用条件。数据集包含来自 109 个不同说话人的 44 小时数据。

items.en: Because the model is not conditioned on text, it generates non-existent but human language-like words in a smooth way with realistic sounding intonations. This is similar to generative models of language or images, where samples look realistic at first glance, but are clearly unnatural upon closer inspection. The lack of long range coherence is partly due to the limited size of the model’s receptive field (about 300 milliseconds), which means it can only remember the last 2–3 phonemes it produced.
items.zh: 由于模型不基于文本条件，它会以平滑的方式生成不存在但类似人类语言的单词，并带有逼真的语调。这类似于语言或图像的生成模型，其中样本乍一看很逼真，但仔细检查明显不自然。缺乏长程一致性部分是由于模型感受野大小有限（约 300 毫秒），这意味着它只能记住最后产生的 2-3 个音素。

items.en: A single WaveNet was able to model speech from any of the speakers by conditioning it on a one-hot encoding of a speaker. This confirms that it is powerful enough to capture the characteristics of all 109 speakers from the dataset in a single model. We observed that adding speakers resulted in better validation set performance compared to training solely on a single speaker. This suggests that WaveNet’s internal representation was shared among multiple speakers.
items.zh: 单个 WaveNet 能够通过将条件设置在说话人的独热编码上来对任何说话人的语音进行建模。这证实了它足够强大，可以在单个模型中捕获数据集中所有 109 个说话人的特征。我们观察到，与仅在单个说话人上训练相比，添加说话人导致更好的验证集性能。这表明 WaveNet 的内部表示在多个说话人之间共享。

items.en: Finally, we observed that the model also picked up on other characteristics in the audio apart from the voice itself. For instance, it also mimicked the acoustics and recording quality, as well as the breathing and mouth movements of the speakers.
items.zh: 最后，我们观察到模型还捕捉到了音频中除了声音本身之外的其他特征。例如，它还模仿了声学和录音质量，以及说话人的呼吸和嘴部运动。

items.en: For the second experiment we looked at TTS. We used the same single-speaker speech databases from which Google’s North American English and Mandarin Chinese TTS systems are built. The North American English dataset contains 24.6 hours of speech data, and the Mandarin Chinese dataset contains 34.8 hours; both were spoken by professional female speakers.
items.zh: 在第二个实验中，我们研究了TTS。我们使用了与谷歌北美英语和普通话TTS系统相同的单说话人语音数据库。北美英语数据集包含24.6小时的语音数据，普通话数据集包含34.8小时；两者均由专业女性说话人录制。

items.en: WaveNets for the TTS task were locally conditioned on linguistic features which were derived from input texts. We also trained WaveNets conditioned on the logarithmic fundamental frequency (log⁡F0subscript𝐹0\log F_{0}) values in addition to the linguistic features. External models predicting log⁡F0subscript𝐹0\log F_{0} values and phone durations from linguistic features were also trained for each language. The receptive field size of the WaveNets was 240 milliseconds. As example-based and model-based speech synthesis baselines, hidden Markov model (HMM)-driven unit selection concatenative (Gonzalvo et al., 2016) and long short-term memory recurrent neural network (LSTM-RNN)-based statistical parametric (Zen et al., 2016) speech synthesizers were built. Since the same datasets and linguistic features were used to train both the baselines and WaveNets, these speech synthesizers could be fairly compared.
items.zh: 用于TTS任务的WaveNet在局部上以从输入文本中提取的语言特征为条件。我们还训练了WaveNet，除了语言特征外，还以对数基频（log F0）值为条件。此外，还为每种语言训练了从语言特征预测log F0值和音素时长的外部模型。WaveNet的感受野大小为240毫秒。作为基于示例和基于模型的语音合成基线，构建了隐马尔可夫模型（HMM）驱动的单元选择拼接式（Gonzalvo等人，2016）和长短期记忆循环神经网络（LSTM-RNN）基于统计参数（Zen等人，2016）的语音合成器。由于基线和WaveNet使用了相同的数据集和语言特征，这些语音合成器可以公平地进行比较。

items.en: To evaluate the performance of WaveNets for the TTS task, subjective paired comparison tests and mean opinion score (MOS) tests were conducted. In the paired comparison tests, after listening to each pair of samples, the subjects were asked to choose which they preferred, though they could choose “neutral” if they did not have any preference. In the MOS tests, after listening to each stimulus, the subjects were asked to rate the naturalness of the stimulus in a five-point Likert scale score (1: Bad, 2: Poor, 3: Fair, 4: Good, 5: Excellent). Please refer to Appendix B for details.
items.zh: 为了评估WaveNet在TTS任务中的性能，进行了主观成对比较测试和平均意见得分（MOS）测试。在成对比较测试中，受试者在听完每对样本后，被问及他们更喜欢哪一个，但如果他们没有偏好，也可以选择“中立”。在MOS测试中，受试者在听完每个刺激后，被要求用五点李克特量表评分（1：差，2：较差，3：一般，4：好，5：优秀）来评价刺激的自然度。详情请参见附录B。

items.en: Fig. 5 shows a selection of the subjective paired comparison test results (see Appendix B for the complete table). It can be seen from the results that WaveNet outperformed the baseline statistical parametric and concatenative speech synthesizers in both languages. We found that WaveNet conditioned on linguistic features could synthesize speech samples with natural segmental quality but sometimes it had unnatural prosody by stressing wrong words in a sentence. This could be due to the long-term dependency of F0subscript𝐹0F_{0} contours: the size of the receptive field of the WaveNet, 240 milliseconds, was not long enough to capture such long-term dependency. WaveNet conditioned on both linguistic features and F0subscript𝐹0F_{0} values did not have this problem: the external F0subscript𝐹0F_{0} prediction model runs at a lower frequency (200 Hz) so it can learn long-range dependencies that exist in F0subscript𝐹0F_{0} contours.
items.zh: 图5展示了部分主观成对比较测试结果（完整表格见附录B）。从结果可以看出，WaveNet在两种语言中均优于基线统计参数和拼接式语音合成器。我们发现，以语言特征为条件的WaveNet能够合成具有自然音段质量的语音样本，但有时会因句子中重读错误单词而产生不自然的韵律。这可能是由于F0轮廓的长期依赖性：WaveNet的感受野大小为240毫秒，不足以捕捉这种长期依赖性。同时以语言特征和F0值为条件的WaveNet没有这个问题：外部F0预测模型以较低频率（200 Hz）运行，因此可以学习F0轮廓中存在的长程依赖关系。

items.capEn: Figure 5: Subjective preference scores (%) of speech samples between (top) two baselines, (middle) two WaveNets, and (bottom) the best baseline and WaveNet. Note that LSTM and Concat correspond to LSTM-RNN-based statistical parametric and HMM-driven unit selection concatenative baseline synthesizers, and WaveNet (L) and WaveNet (L+F) correspond to the WaveNet conditioned on linguistic features only and that conditioned on both linguistic features and log⁡F0subscript𝐹0\log F_{0} values.
items.capZh: 图5：语音样本的主观偏好得分（%），（上）两个基线之间，（中）两个WaveNet之间，（下）最佳基线与WaveNet之间。注意，LSTM和Concat分别对应基于LSTM-RNN的统计参数和HMM驱动的单元选择拼接式基线合成器，WaveNet (L)和WaveNet (L+F)分别对应仅以语言特征为条件的WaveNet和同时以语言特征和log F0值为条件的WaveNet。

items.en: For out third set of experiments we trained WaveNets to model two music datasets:
items.zh: 在第三组实验中，我们训练WaveNet对两个音乐数据集进行建模：

items.en: the MagnaTagATune dataset (Law & Von Ahn, 2009), which consists of about 200 hours of music audio. Each 29-second clip is annotated with tags from a set of 188, which describe the genre, instrumentation, tempo, volume and mood of the music.
items.zh: MagnaTagATune数据集（Law & Von Ahn, 2009），包含约200小时的音乐音频。每个29秒的片段都附有来自188个标签集的标签，描述音乐的流派、乐器、节奏、音量和情绪。

items.en: the YouTube piano dataset, which consists of about 60 hours of solo piano music obtained from YouTube videos. Because it is constrained to a single instrument, it is considerably easier to model.
items.zh: YouTube钢琴数据集，包含从YouTube视频中获取的约60小时钢琴独奏音乐。由于仅限于单一乐器，因此建模起来要容易得多。

items.en: Although it is difficult to quantitatively evaluate these models, a subjective evaluation is possible by listening to the samples they produce. We found that enlarging the receptive field was crucial to obtain samples that sounded musical. Even with a receptive field of several seconds, the models did not enforce long-range consistency which resulted in second-to-second variations in genre, instrumentation, volume and sound quality. Nevertheless, the samples were often harmonic and aesthetically pleasing, even when produced by unconditional models.
items.zh: 尽管难以对这些模型进行定量评估，但通过聆听它们生成的样本可以进行主观评估。我们发现，扩大感受野对于获得听起来有音乐感的样本至关重要。即使感受野达到几秒，模型也没有强制执行长程一致性，导致流派、乐器、音量和音质在秒级变化。尽管如此，即使是无条件模型生成的样本也常常是和谐且美观的。

items.en: Of particular interest are conditional music models, which can generate music given a set of tags specifying e.g. genre or instruments. Similarly to conditional speech models, we insert biases that depend on a binary vector representation of the tags associated with each training clip. This makes it possible to control various aspects of the output of the model when sampling, by feeding in a binary vector that encodes the desired properties of the samples. We have trained such models on the MagnaTagATune dataset; although the tag data bundled with the dataset was relatively noisy and had many omissions, after cleaning it up by merging similar tags and removing those with too few associated clips, we found this approach to work reasonably well.
items.zh: 特别令人感兴趣的是条件音乐模型，它们可以根据指定流派或乐器等标签集生成音乐。与条件语音模型类似，我们插入依赖于每个训练片段关联标签的二进制向量表示的偏置。这使得在采样时，通过输入编码样本所需属性的二进制向量，可以控制模型输出的各个方面。我们已经在MagnaTagATune数据集上训练了这样的模型；尽管数据集附带的标签数据相对嘈杂且有许多遗漏，但在通过合并相似标签和删除关联片段过少的标签进行清理后，我们发现这种方法效果相当不错。

items.en: Although WaveNet was designed as a generative model, it can straightforwardly be adapted to discriminative audio tasks such as speech recognition.
items.zh: 尽管WaveNet被设计为生成模型，但它可以轻松地适应于判别性音频任务，如语音识别。

items.en: Traditionally, speech recognition research has largely focused on using log mel-filterbank energies or mel-frequency cepstral coefficients (MFCCs), but has been moving to raw audio recently (Palaz et al., 2013; Tüske et al., 2014; Hoshen et al., 2015; Sainath et al., 2015). Recurrent neural networks such as LSTM-RNNs (Hochreiter & Schmidhuber, 1997) have been a key component in these new speech classification pipelines, because they allow for building models with long range contexts. With WaveNets we have shown that layers of dilated convolutions allow the receptive field to grow longer in a much cheaper way than using LSTM units.
items.zh: 传统上，语音识别研究主要关注使用对数梅尔滤波器组能量或梅尔频率倒谱系数（MFCC），但最近已转向原始音频（Palaz等人，2013；Tüske等人，2014；Hoshen等人，2015；Sainath等人，2015）。循环神经网络如LSTM-RNN（Hochreiter & Schmidhuber, 1997）已成为这些新语音分类流程中的关键组件，因为它们允许构建具有长程上下文的模型。通过WaveNet，我们展示了扩张卷积层能够以比使用LSTM单元更廉价的方式使感受野增长得更长。

items.en: As a last experiment we looked at speech recognition with WaveNets on the TIMIT (Garofolo et al., 1993) dataset. For this task we added a mean-pooling layer after the dilated convolutions that aggregated the activations to coarser frames spanning 10 milliseconds (160×\times downsampling). The pooling layer was followed by a few non-causal convolutions. We trained WaveNet with two loss terms, one to predict the next sample and one to classify the frame, the model generalized better than with a single loss and achieved 18.818.818.8 PER on the test set, which is to our knowledge the best score obtained from a model trained directly on raw audio on TIMIT.
items.zh: 作为最后一个实验，我们在TIMIT（Garofolo等人，1993）数据集上研究了使用WaveNet进行语音识别。为此，我们在扩张卷积之后添加了一个平均池化层，将激活聚合到跨度为10毫秒（160倍下采样）的较粗帧上。池化层之后是几个非因果卷积。我们使用两个损失项训练WaveNet，一个用于预测下一个样本，一个用于对帧进行分类，该模型比使用单一损失泛化得更好，并在测试集上达到了18.8的PER，据我们所知，这是在TIMIT上直接对原始音频训练的最佳得分。

items.en: This paper has presented WaveNet, a deep generative model of audio data that operates directly at the waveform level. WaveNets are autoregressive and combine causal filters with dilated convolutions to allow their receptive fields to grow exponentially with depth, which is important to model the long-range temporal dependencies in audio signals. We have shown how WaveNets can be conditioned on other inputs in a global (e.g. speaker identity) or local way (e.g. linguistic features). When applied to TTS, WaveNets produced samples that outperform the current best TTS systems in subjective naturalness. Finally, WaveNets showed very promising results when applied to music audio modeling and speech recognition.
items.zh: 本文介绍了WaveNet，一种直接在波形级别操作的音频数据深度生成模型。WaveNet是自回归的，结合了因果滤波器和扩张卷积，使其感受野随深度呈指数增长，这对于建模音频信号中的长程时间依赖性非常重要。我们展示了如何以全局（如说话人身份）或局部（如语言特征）方式将WaveNet条件化于其他输入。当应用于TTS时，WaveNet生成的样本在主观自然度上优于当前最佳的TTS系统。最后，WaveNet在音乐音频建模和语音识别方面显示出非常有前景的结果。

items.en: The authors would like to thank Lasse Espeholt, Jeffrey De Fauw and Grzegorz Swirszcz for their inputs, Adam Cain, Max Cant and Adrian Bolton for their help with artwork, Helen King, Steven Gaffney and Steve Crossan for helping to manage the project, Faith Mackinder for help with preparing the blogpost, James Besley for legal support and Demis Hassabis for managing the project and his inputs.
items.zh: 作者感谢Lasse Espeholt、Jeffrey De Fauw和Grzegorz Swirszcz的贡献，Adam Cain、Max Cant和Adrian Bolton在艺术创作上的帮助，Helen King、Steven Gaffney和Steve Crossan在项目管理上的帮助，Faith Mackinder在准备博客文章上的帮助，James Besley的法律支持，以及Demis Hassabis在项目管理和贡献上的帮助。
